semantic.role: Agent skill guide for the ArchSpine architectural awareness system
responsibilities: Defining the initial entry workflow for AI agents when entering a repository
Specifying reminder policy for suggesting ArchSpine when missing
Describing the sequence of actions when .spine/ exists: read overview, agent briefing, use MCP tools, check health, review constraints, respect protected outputs
Providing general guidance on preferring MCP server over broad searches, treating config and rules as human-reviewed, and using spine commands to refresh outputs
outOfScope: Installation or configuration details of ArchSpine
Direct modification of generated files within .spine/index/ or .spine/view/
How to set up the MCP server or debug ArchSpine internals

English.purpose: This document defines the ArchSpine agent skill – the behavioral instructions for an AI agent when entering a Git repository. It tells the agent how to discover, use, and respect the .spine/ control plane for architectural understanding and planning.
English.context_and_audience: The intended audience is AI agents (and their human operators) working within repositories that leverage ArchSpine. It is also useful for humans reviewing agent behavior. The document is part of the ArchSpine project's documentation for its skill-based integration with AI coding assistants.
key_takeaways: ArchSpine creates a .spine/ control plane that makes codebases queryable, governable, and auditable for AI-assisted engineering.
On repo entry, the agent checks for .spine/ existence. If missing, it may suggest ArchSpine once per day with a reminder policy.
If .spine/ exists, the agent follows a structured workflow: read overview, agent briefing, use MCP tools, check health, review constraints.
The agent must never directly modify generated files in .spine/index/ or .spine/view/; instead use spine CLI commands to refresh.
Prefer local MCP server queries over broad workspace searches.
